title: CLI (typer)
description: Command-line interface for ingesting documents, querying the graph, and viewing stats. Built with typer + rich.

title: Implement ingest command
description: Wire up the full ingestion pipeline (parse → chunk → embed → extract entities → store in kuzu) behind 'pdf-rag ingest <path>'. Support file and directory input.

title: Implement search command
description: 'pdf-rag search <query>' embeds the query, runs vector search on Chunks, retrieves context, calls Claude API for answer. Stream output to terminal with rich.

title: Implement stats command
description: 'pdf-rag stats' queries kuzu for counts of Papers, Authors, Topics, Chunks, Institutions, Venues and prints a rich table.

title: arXiv integration: find related/similar papers for a selected document
description:
Given a paper already in the graph, search arXiv for related work and optionally ingest results.

Approach:
1. ArxivClient: wraps GET https://export.arxiv.org/api/query, parses Atom XML response, enforces 3s rate limit between calls
2. Search strategies (all composable):
   - by_topic: search ti: + abs: using paper's topic nodes from graph
   - by_author: search au: using paper's author nodes
   - by_arxiv_id: fetch via id_list if paper has arxiv_id stored, then search by primary category
3. Re-rank results by embedding similarity against the selected paper's chunks (already in kuzu)
4. CLI command: pdf-rag related <paper-id-or-file> [--top-k 10] [--strategy topic|author|category|all]
5. Server endpoint: GET /api/papers/{id}/related-arxiv?strategy=all&top_k=10
6. Optional: ingest top-N results directly (pdf-rag related ... --ingest)

Response schema per result: {arxiv_id, title, authors, abstract, published, categories, pdf_url, similarity_score}
Attribution: include arXiv acknowledgement in CLI output and API response.
close_reason: ArxivClient, find_related(), CLI related command, REST endpoint — all tests passing

title: News ingestion: poll free APIs, summarise, store in graph
description: Add a scheduled background system that polls free news APIs (GDELT, Hacker News, Finnhub, Google News RSS, Semantic Scholar), extracts full text via Trafilatura/Newspaper4k, generates LLM summaries and topic tags, and stores articles as NewsArticle nodes in the kuzu graph with Author, Topic, and Source edges. A new News feed view in the frontend displays the stream with topic pills, source, and timestamp. See feature_docs/free_news_apis.md for API details.

title: Embedder: LM Studio /v1/embeddings backend (Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B)
description: When local LLM server is reachable, route embedding calls through LM Studio's /v1/embeddings endpoint instead of loading sentence-transformers in-process. Falls back to sentence-transformers (CPU) when local server is unreachable. Config: LOCAL_EMBEDDING_MODEL (default Qwen/Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B). No change to EMBEDDING_DIM (still 1024). Embedder picks backend at first encode() call.
close_reason: LM Studio /v1/embeddings backend with auto-probe, falls back to sentence-transformers

title: Save ingested files to a configured destination folder
description: Files uploaded via POST /api/ingest are currently written to a temp file and deleted after ingestion. Instead, copy them to a configured INGEST_DIR folder (default ~/.pdf_rag/documents/) so the user has one place for all ingested docs. The paper's file_path in the graph should point to the permanent location. Needs: INGEST_DIR config constant, update ingest.py to copy before ingesting, CLI ingest should also copy if source is outside INGEST_DIR (optional/configurable).

title: RAG Query Pipeline
description: End-to-end retrieval-augmented generation: embed query, vector search chunks, graph-expand context (related topics, authors), assemble prompt, call Claude.

title: Implement chunk retrieval
description: Given a query embedding, retrieve top-k Chunks via kuzu vector search. Return chunks with their Paper provenance.

title: Implement graph context expansion
description: For retrieved chunks, expand context via graph: fetch related Topics (RELATED_TO), paper Authors, citing Papers. Add to prompt context.

title: Implement Claude answer generation
description: Assemble retrieved chunks + graph context into a prompt. Call Claude via anthropic SDK (streaming). Return answer with source citations.

title: REST endpoints for news feed and article detail
description: Create src/pdf_rag/server/routers/news.py. Endpoints: GET /api/news (paginated list, filters: source, topic, since, limit=20), GET /api/news/{id} (full article detail with topics/authors), GET /api/news/sources (list configured sources with last-poll time and article count). Register router in main.py.

title: OOM crash during ingest: CUDA out of memory when loading multiple models
description:
Running 'pdf-rag ingest' crashes with CUDA OOM when loading Embedder and EntityExtractor sequentially. GPU (11.62 GiB) fills up because models are loaded back-to-back without releasing VRAM between loads.

Error: OutOfMemoryError: CUDA out of memory. Tried to allocate 118.00 MiB. GPU 0 has a total capacity of 11.62 GiB of which 81.94 MiB is free. 8.91 GiB allocated by PyTorch.

Root causes to investigate:
1. SentenceTransformer (Qwen3 1024-dim) + GLiNER both loaded into VRAM simultaneously
2. No explicit device placement — both default to CUDA
3. No torch.cuda.empty_cache() between model loads

Fix options:
- Force one or both models to CPU (GLINER is fast enough on CPU)
- Add PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF=expandable_segments:True support
- Load models sequentially with cache clear between loads
- Add config flag: EMBEDDING_DEVICE, GLINER_DEVICE (default cpu for GLiNER)
close_reason: EMBEDDING_DEVICE/GLINER_DEVICE config added; EntityExtractor now accepts device param defaulting to cpu; all tests passing

title: Graph: arXiv search trigger from node context menu (Topic, Author, Paper)
description: Right-click context menu on graph nodes: Topic → 'Search arXiv for this topic' (terms=[topic name]), Author → 'Search arXiv by this author' (author=name), Paper → 'Find related on arXiv' (uses existing /papers/{id}/related-arxiv endpoint). Calls POST /arxiv/search, drops ghost nodes into graph via ghost node layer. Results panel shows arXiv attribution.
close_reason: Implemented in GraphExplorer rewrite — right-click cxttap triggers arXiv search, ghost node detail panel shows metadata + ingest action

title: Service setup: systemd (Linux) + launchd/app (macOS) with folder watching
description: Provide a setup script or install command that registers pdf-rag-serve as a background service. Linux: systemd unit file. macOS: launchd plist or a menu-bar app wrapper. Future: watch a list of configurable folders and auto-ingest new files as they appear (inotify/FSEvents). Config: list of watched folders stored in ~/.pdf_rag/config.toml.

title: GraphStore methods for writing NewsArticle nodes and edges
description: Add to src/pdf_rag/graph/store.py: add_news_article(), add_source(), link_news_topic(), link_news_author(), link_news_source(). Follow existing MERGE upsert pattern. add_news_article takes (id, title, summary, url, full_text, published_at, fetched_at, source). Use article URL hash as id.

title: Generate semantic title from abstract when docling finds no title
description: Papers without a proper title (e.g. PDFs where Docling can't extract one) fall back to the filename stem, which is unhelpful. When doc.title is empty and doc.abstract or doc.sections exist, use the LLM to generate a short descriptive title (≤12 words) from the first ~500 chars of content. This affects pipeline.py and potentially a helper in llm.py.

title: News feed page with topic pills, source badge, and timestamp
description: Create frontend/src/pages/NewsFeed.tsx. Left panel: scrollable article list with title, 2-line summary preview, source badge (coloured by domain), relative timestamp, and topic pills. Right panel: full article detail with summary, topic chips (clickable → /topics/:id), author chips (clickable → /authors/:id), source link, and published date. Add /news route to App.tsx and Newspaper icon to sidebar nav.

title: Graph: ghost node detail panel and ingest action
description: Clicking a ghost Paper node opens a detail panel showing arXiv metadata (title, authors, abstract, categories, year, similarity score). Panel has an Ingest button that calls POST /arxiv/ingest. On success: ghost node and its ghost edges are replaced by real kuzu-backed nodes fetched from /papers/{id}. Ghost Author/Topic nodes that match existing nodes should highlight the existing node instead of creating a duplicate.
close_reason: Implemented in GraphExplorer rewrite — right-click cxttap triggers arXiv search, ghost node detail panel shows metadata + ingest action

title: LLM summarisation and topic tagging for news articles
description: Create src/pdf_rag/news/enricher.py. Given full text, call generate_title() for a headline (if missing) and a new generate_summary(text) function (3-sentence max) using the existing llm.py backend. Extract topics via GLiNER EntityExtractor (reuse existing extraction/entities.py). Normalise topics via existing normalise_topics(). Return EnrichedArticle dataclass with summary, topics list, and authors list.

title: Store arXiv ID on Paper nodes in graph schema
description: The citation extractor already parses arXiv IDs from references but they are not stored in the graph. Add arxiv_id STRING column to the Paper table in schema.py, update add_paper() in store.py to accept it, update pipeline.py to pass it through from ParsedDocument metadata. Needed as prerequisite for the arXiv related-paper search (pdf-rag-0gb).

title: Show NewsArticle nodes in graph views linked to existing Topic/Author nodes
description: Add NewsArticle as a node type in the graph overview endpoint (/api/graph/overview). Add ICON_NEWS (newspaper icon) to cyStylesheet.ts. NewsArticle nodes appear in GraphExplorer and TopicMap as a new node type (slate/grey-blue colour), expanding to reveal their shared topics and authors which link back to existing Paper nodes. This allows cross-referencing papers and news articles on shared topics.

title: Full-text extraction from article URLs via Trafilatura
description: Create src/pdf_rag/news/extractor.py wrapping trafilatura.fetch_url + trafilatura.extract. Accepts a RawArticle URL, returns cleaned full text (or falls back to snippet if extraction fails or URL is paywalled). Add trafilatura to pyproject.toml dependencies. Rate-limit fetches with a configurable delay (default 1s between requests per domain).

title: Background ingestion queue: persist upload progress across page navigation
description: Currently the IngestUI loses progress when the user navigates away. Ingest jobs should run in a background queue (server-side or React context) so that returning to /ingest shows live status of ongoing and completed uploads. Likely requires a server-side job store (in-memory dict keyed by job ID) and a GET /api/ingest/jobs endpoint for polling, plus a React context so the queue state is global.

title: Author detail graph: clicking an author opens their paper/co-author graph
description: Clicking an author node (in GraphExplorer or future author list) should open a focused subgraph of that author's papers and co-authors. GET /api/authors/{id}/papers and GET /api/authors/{id}/coauthors endpoints already exist.

title: Paper detail: clicking topic/author chips navigates to their graph
description: In the PaperBrowser detail panel, topic tags and author names should be clickable links that navigate to the respective topic or author detail graph (linking to the features above).

title: Graph Store (kuzu)
description: All read/write operations against the kuzu embedded graph database. Covers node/edge creation, vector similarity search, and graph traversal queries for the RAG pipeline.

title: Implement GraphStore node writers
description: Implement add_paper, add_author, add_institution, add_venue, add_topic in GraphStore. Handle upsert (no duplicate nodes). TDD.

title: Implement GraphStore edge writers
description: Implement link_author_paper, link_paper_topic, link_chunk_topic, link_paper_venue, link_paper_cites in GraphStore. TDD.

title: Implement vector similarity search
description: Use kuzu's built-in vector search on Chunk.embedding to find top-k similar chunks for a query embedding.

title: Implement graph traversal queries
description: Cypher queries for: papers by author, papers by topic, related topics (RELATED_TO edges), co-author network, citation graph.

title: Ingest UI: parallel file uploads
description: Currently the IngestUI submits files one at a time sequentially. Switch to parallel fetch calls (Promise.allSettled) so multiple files are ingested concurrently. Keep per-file status updates.

title: Batch PDF ingestion: limit concurrent memory usage
description:
Currently the CLI ingests all files sequentially but loads all models into memory once. The issue is that docling + embedder + GLiNER together use significant RAM/VRAM, and ingesting many large PDFs back-to-back can OOM.

Two complementary strategies:
1. **File count batching**: process N files at a time (e.g. 3), releasing parsed document memory between batches
2. **Size-based batching**: accumulate files up to a total size threshold (e.g. 50MB) per batch, then flush

Implementation:
- Add --batch-size INT and --batch-mb FLOAT options to 'pdf-rag ingest' CLI command
- In cli.py ingest(), chunk the file list into batches before the processing loop
- Models (embedder, extractor) are still loaded once and reused across batches
- Between batches, explicitly del prepared documents and call gc.collect() to release parsed PDF memory
- Default: batch-size=3 (safe for most hardware), batch-mb=0 (disabled by default, user opts in)
- If both are set, use whichever limit triggers first

No changes needed to pipeline.py or IngestManager — batching is purely a CLI concern.

title: Embedded PDF viewer in paper detail panel
description: When viewing a paper's detail in PaperBrowser, add a collapsible embedded PDF viewer using the browser's native <embed> or PDF.js. Only shown when the file_path is a .pdf accessible from the server. May require a new GET /api/papers/{id}/pdf endpoint that streams the file from disk.

title: POST /arxiv/search endpoint for keyword/topic/author search
description: Add a generic arXiv search endpoint that doesn't require a paper_id. POST /arxiv/search accepts {terms: list[str], author: str, top_k: int} and returns ArxivResult list. Reuses existing ArxivClient. Needed by frontend graph arXiv integration.

title: Local LLM backend: Ollama/LM Studio as alternative to Anthropic API
description: Allow users to configure the answer-generation backend as either Anthropic Claude (current default) or a local OpenAI-compatible server (Ollama, LM Studio, etc.). The choice should be made via config/env var (e.g. LLM_BACKEND=anthropic|local, LOCAL_LLM_BASE_URL, LOCAL_LLM_MODEL). The retriever should route to the correct backend at call time with no code changes required from the user. Both paths must produce a RetrievalResult with identical fields.
close_reason: auto backend, probe_local, list_local_models, qwen3.5-9b default, CLI models command, /api/models endpoint

title: Ghost node click: populate detail panel with abstract/authors/citations + ingest button
description: Clicking a ghost paper/author/topic node does not show abstract, authors, citations or any detail. The right-hand panel should pop out showing: for Paper ghosts — abstract, authors, topics, citation count, arXiv link, Ingest button; for Author ghosts — papers list, co-authors; for Topic ghosts — papers and authors. Ingest button should live in this panel, not scattered elsewhere.

title: Fix SPA deep-link 404: catch-all /app/* route returns index.html
description: Hard-refreshing on any /app/* route (e.g. /app/graph, /app/papers) returns 404 because FastAPI tries to serve a static file that doesn't exist. Add a catch-all route that returns index.html for any unmatched /app/* path so React Router handles client-side navigation correctly.

title: POST /arxiv/ingest endpoint — download and ingest by arxiv_id
description: Server-side endpoint that accepts {arxiv_id: str}, downloads the PDF from arxiv.org, runs ingest_document(), and returns the new paper_id. Needed to convert ghost nodes to real nodes from the frontend.

title: Add NewsArticle node and news edges to kuzu schema
description: Add NewsArticle node table (id, title, summary, url, full_text, published_at TIMESTAMP, fetched_at TIMESTAMP, source STRING, arxiv_id STRING) and edges: NEWS_DISCUSSES (NewsArticle→Topic), NEWS_AUTHORED (Author→NewsArticle), FROM_SOURCE (NewsArticle→Source). Add Source node table (id, name, url). Migration via _add_column_if_missing pattern in schema.py.

title: DB management: truncate or delete the graph database from the UI
description: Add a Settings or Admin page (or section in the sidebar) with two dangerous actions: (1) Truncate — delete all nodes/edges/chunks but keep the DB file; (2) Delete DB — remove the kuzu database entirely and start fresh. Both require a double-confirmation step (type 'confirm' or click twice). Backend needs POST /api/admin/truncate and DELETE /api/admin/db endpoints. Should show current DB stats before confirming.

title: Disable auto-zoom during node drag
description: When dragging a node the viewport currently zooms into the node and its immediate children. The viewport should not zoom unless the user explicitly scrolls with the mouse. Need to investigate what is triggering the zoom during drag (possibly Cytoscape's fit/pan-to-node behaviour or a runLayout call triggered by drag events) and suppress it.

title: Serialise kuzu writes via single DB-writer thread
description: Parallel ingest threads all open GraphStore concurrently, causing kuzu DB corruption. Fix by splitting ingest into two phases: (1) parse/embed/extract runs in parallel worker threads, (2) store-to-graph runs serially in a dedicated DB-writer thread via a queue.

title: Entity Extraction & Normalisation
description: Extract authors, institutions, venues, topics, and citations from parsed documents using GLiNER2. Normalise entities using rapidfuzz (authors) and embedding clustering (topics).

title: Implement GLiNER2 entity extractor
description: Wrap knowledgator/gliner-multitask-large-v0.5 to extract Author, Institution, Venue, Topic, Citation entities from chunk text. Return typed entity objects.

title: Implement author normalisation
description: Deduplicate author name variants (e.g. 'J. Smith' vs 'John Smith') using rapidfuzz fuzzy matching. Produce canonical Author nodes.

title: Implement topic normalisation
description: Cluster extracted topic/concept strings by embedding similarity (threshold 0.85 from config) into canonical Topic nodes. Handle synonym merging.

title: Implement citation extraction
description: Extract in-text citations and reference list entries from DoclingDocument. Resolve to Paper nodes where possible (by DOI or title match).

title: Bug: view PDF downloads instead of displaying in iframe (Firefox)
description: Firefox downloads the PDF instead of rendering it in the iframe viewer. Chrome may work due to its built-in PDF renderer. Need to investigate the endpoint serving the PDF - content-type, content-disposition headers, and whether Firefox requires a different approach (e.g. PDF.js or blob URL).
close_reason: Fixed: removed filename= from FileResponse which was adding Content-Disposition: attachment, causing Firefox to download instead of inline render

title: Ingestion Pipeline
description: Parse documents (PDF, DOCX, MD, HTML, LaTeX) using docling, chunk them section-aware, embed with Qwen3, and store chunks in kuzu graph DB.

title: Implement docling-based document parser
description: Wrap docling to parse PDF/DOCX/MD/HTML/LaTeX into a normalised DoclingDocument. Expose parse(path) -> Document interface. TDD.

title: Implement section-aware chunker
description: Split DoclingDocument into Chunk objects preserving section headings (Abstract, Methods, Results etc). Respect CHUNK_SIZE and CHUNK_OVERLAP from config.

title: Implement sentence-transformers embedder
description: Wrap sentence-transformers + Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B to produce embeddings for Chunk text. Expose embed(texts) -> list[list[float]].

title: Store chunks and embeddings in kuzu
description: Implement GraphStore.add_chunk() to persist Chunk nodes with embeddings using kuzu's vector column support.

title: Visualisation Server
description: Local Python-based server to enable visual human interaction with the graph RAG data. Allows browsing authors, topics, papers and their relationships visually. Details TBD — discuss before implementation.

title: FastAPI server setup
description: Scaffold FastAPI app with CORS, static file serving, uvicorn entry point. Wire into pyproject.toml as second CLI entry point 'pdf-rag-serve'. Add pytest-asyncio and httpx as dev deps for API testing.

title: Graph API endpoints
description: REST endpoints for graph traversal: GET /api/authors/{id}/papers, GET /api/topics/{id}/papers, GET /api/topics/{id}/related, GET /api/authors/{id}/coauthors, GET /api/papers/{id}/citations. All backed by GraphStore traversal methods.

title: Search API endpoint
description: POST /api/search — accepts query string, runs vector search on Chunks via GraphStore, expands graph context (related topics, authors), returns ranked results with provenance. GET /api/ask — streams Claude answer with citations via SSE.

title: Graph explorer UI
description: Cytoscape.js network view showing Paper, Author, Topic nodes and their edges. Click a node to load its neighbours. Support force-directed and hierarchical layouts. Filter by node type. Built with React + Vite + Tailwind.
close_reason: All four pages built and wired; Search page added as bonus

title: Paper browser UI
description: Searchable list of ingested papers with title, authors, year, topics. Click paper to open detail panel showing abstract, chunks, cited-by, topics discussed. Pagination.
close_reason: All four pages built and wired; Search page added as bonus

title: Topic map UI
description: Clustered view of Topic nodes sized by paper count, connected by RELATED_TO edges with weight. Click topic to filter paper browser. Shows co-occurrence clusters.
close_reason: All four pages built and wired; Search page added as bonus

title: Ingest UI
description: Form to submit a file path or directory for ingestion. Shows real-time progress via SSE (parsing → chunking → embedding → entity extraction → graph storage). Displays summary on completion.
close_reason: All four pages built and wired; Search page added as bonus

title: Graph explorer: truncate long node labels
description: Paper titles in the Cytoscape graph overflow and overlap neighbouring nodes. Truncate labels to ~40 chars in the node style (Cytoscape text-overflow + text-max-width), show full title in the selected node side panel.

title: Improve topic normalisation + add LLM paper summary and keywords
description:
Two related improvements to ingestion quality:

1. Topic cleaning & normalisation
   - Strip extra whitespace, punctuation artifacts, and fix capitalisation before fuzzy clustering
   - Lower the fuzzy threshold slightly for topics (0.80 vs 0.85) since short phrases match less precisely
   - Title-case canonical topic names consistently
   - Filter out single-character and purely numeric tokens

2. LLM summary + keywords per paper
   - Generate a 2-3 sentence summary of the paper stored on the Paper node (summary field already exists in schema)
   - Extract 5-10 keyword phrases and store as additional Topic nodes linked via DISCUSSES
   - Display summary in PaperBrowser detail panel (above or replacing raw abstract)
   - Use local LLM with thinking mode disabled; fall back to Anthropic if local unavailable

Implementation notes:
   - Add clean_topic(text: str) -> str helper in normaliser.py
   - Add generate_summary(text: str) -> str and extract_keywords(text: str) -> list[str] in llm.py
   - Call both in pipeline.prepare_document() after entity extraction
   - Summary and keywords should use a non-thinking model prompt (short, structured output)

title: Topic detail graph: clicking a topic opens its paper/neighbour graph
description: Currently the Topics page shows a static topic-only graph. Clicking a topic node should open a detail view showing that topic's connected papers, authors, and related topics in a focused subgraph. Should reuse the Cytoscape component with expand-neighbours capability. API endpoint GET /api/topics/{id}/papers and GET /api/topics/{id}/related already exist.

title: Background scheduler for periodic news polling
description: Create src/pdf_rag/news/scheduler.py. Use APScheduler (BackgroundScheduler) or a simple threading.Timer loop. Configurable poll interval per source (default: GDELT/HN every 15min, Finnhub every 30min, RSS every 1hr, SemanticScholar every 6hr). Each run: collect → extract → enrich → store via serial DB-writer queue (reuse IngestManager pattern from server/routers/ingest.py). Store last-seen article timestamps to avoid re-processing. Start scheduler on app startup via FastAPI lifespan event.

title: Graph: ghost node layer for unpopulated arXiv results
description: Frontend graph component gets a ghostNodes layer (separate from kuzu-backed nodes). arXiv search results render as ghost Paper nodes (dashed border, cloud/download icon, greyed) plus ghost Author and Topic nodes from arXiv metadata categories/authors. Ghost nodes use temp IDs like arxiv:{arxiv_id}. Ghost edges connect ghost Paper to ghost Author/Topic nodes. State is purely frontend — nothing written to kuzu until ingested.

title: Implement news collector modules (GDELT, HN, Finnhub, Google RSS, Semantic Scholar)
description: Create src/pdf_rag/news/collectors/ package with one module per source. Each collector implements a collect() -> list[RawArticle] interface returning title, url, snippet, source, published_at. Sources: GDELT (no key, 250 results/query), Hacker News Algolia API (no key, unlimited), Finnhub company-news endpoint (free key, 60 req/min), Google News RSS (no key, topic + search feeds), Semantic Scholar (no key, 100 req/5min). RawArticle is a dataclass in pdf_rag/news/models.py.
